events.title: Pongal Begins
events.description: Pongal is a major South Indian harvest festival celebrated over four days, marked by thanksgiving rituals, cooking of the traditional pongal dish, kolam decorations, and vibrant cultural festivities.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Pongal is a deeply rooted South Indian harvest festival — genZScore 1-3. Primarily Tamil and broader South Indian community. Women dress in traditional silk sarees, pavadai davani, or ethnic sets in bright harvest-inspired colors — turmeric yellow, earthy orange, deep red, and green. Men in traditional dhotis, veshtis, or kurta-pyjama sets. The mood is joyful, familial, and devotional. Jewellery is traditional gold-heavy — temple jewellery is iconic for this festival. No western influence — full ethnic with some younger GenZ fusion possible on day 2-3 of the festival.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: South Indian harvest vibes — turmeric yellow and earthy orange are THE colors of Pongal. Women in silk-feel cotton sarees with zari borders, pavadai davani for younger girls, or bright ethnic sets. Men in crisp white or off-white veshtis with a kurta or traditional angavastram. Temple jewellery is iconic — layered gold-tone necklaces, jhumkas, kamarbandh, and vanki (armlet). Kolam-inspired geometric and floral patterns in fabric are a great catalog match. Kids' ethnic wear is highly relevant — onesies and ethnic sets for little ones. Day 1 is most traditional, later days can allow fusion cotton co-ords with ethnic accessories.

app.headline: Pongal Drip, No Cap.
lines: Harvest season but make it silk saree and temple jewels.
Yellow, gold, and a whole lot of tradition calling.
Your Pongal look is one tap away — go get it.

social_media.headline: Pongalo Pongal, But Make It Fashion.
lines: POV: you showed up to Pongal in a silk saree and temple jewellery. Icon.
Turmeric yellow fit check — the most auspicious color of the year.

events.title: Lohri
events.description: Lohri is a vibrant North Indian harvest festival celebrated with bonfires, folk music, bhangra and giddha dancing, rewri, and festive gatherings marking the end of winter solstice.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Lohri is a North Indian harvest festival — primarily Punjabi community. genZScore sits at 2-4. It's an outdoor bonfire night event which adds a practical layer — the cold January night means layering is key. Women in bright phulkari dupattas, salwar suits, lehengas. Men in kurta-pyjama, pathani suits, or fusion ethnic. Colors are bold and warm — deep reds, pinks, oranges, mustard yellows reflecting the bonfire energy. Younger GenZ crowd may do fusion — ethnic top with jeans, or a phulkari dupatta styled over western. Jewellery is traditional Punjabi — oxidised silver, chunky pieces, jhumkas. Layering with shawls and jackets is essential for the winter night bonfire setting.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Bonfire night in January — layering is non-negotiable. Lead with phulkari-embroidered dupattas and salwar suits in deep reds, mustard, and fuchsia for women. Lehengas with mirror-work or gota-patti detailing are perfect for the dance floor around the fire. Men in classic kurta-pyjama or pathani suits — mustard and ivory are strong. GenZ fusion angle — phulkari dupatta over a plain kurta with straight-fit jeans works beautifully. Chunky oxidised silver jewellery, jhumkas, and statement necklaces are the Lohri jewellery signature. Shawls and embroidered jackets are functional AND stylish for the cold. Kids ethnic sets are highly relevant.

app.headline: Bonfire Ready, Fully Ethnic.
lines: Phulkari, fire, and your most iconic ethnic fit.
Cold night, warm colors — dress the Lohri way.
Your lehenga moment is literally tonight.

social_media.headline: Lohri Fit Check — Go.
lines: POV: showed up in phulkari and stole the whole bonfire.
Mustard kurta + mirror dupatta > every other fit tonight.

events.title: Makar Sankranti
events.description: Makar Sankranti is a pan-Indian harvest festival marking the sun's transition into Capricorn, celebrated with kite flying, sesame sweets, holy dips, bonfires, and regional customs across the country.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Makar Sankranti is a pan-India festival with strong regional variations — genZScore 2-4. It's a daytime outdoor festival — kite flying on terraces and open grounds means practical yet festive dressing. The mood is joyful, colorful, and family-oriented. In Gujarat it's Uttarayan — very colorful, kite-fest energy with young crowds. In Maharashtra women wear traditional black sarees (the one day black is auspicious). In Bengal it's Poush Sankranti with white and red cotton sarees. Colors are multi and vibrant — mirroring the kites in the sky. Ethnic is primary, fusion acceptable for younger crowd. Outdoor daytime setting means breathable cotton fabrics. Jewellery is moderate — not too heavy, functional for an active day.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Pan-India festival with regional color codes — lean into all of them. Maharashtra crowd: black sarees with gold zari borders are the signature, auspicious and stunning. Gujarat/Uttarayan crowd: maximally colorful — bandhej prints, mirror-work, bright co-ords, kite-inspired color-blocking. Bengal crowd: red-bordered white cotton sarees. Men in kurta-pyjama or dhoti-kurta in white, yellow, or off-white. GenZ fusion angle works well here — ethnic printed co-ords, bandhej crop tops with palazzos, or a colorful dupatta over western separates. Breathable cotton is essential — it's an outdoor daytime event. Jewellery should be moderate and functional, not too heavy. Kids ethnic sets and onesies are very relevant.

app.headline: Kites Up, Fits Even Higher.
lines: Sankranti colors hit different on a rooftop.
Black saree in Maharashtra, bandhej in Gujarat — we have both.
Dress for the kites, stay for the tilgul.

social_media.headline: Outfit Flying Higher Than Kites.
lines: POV: your Sankranti fit is more colorful than every kite on the terrace.
Black saree energy vs bandhej chaos — which Sankranti are you celebrating?

events.title: BLR Hubba 2026 Begins
events.description: BLR Hubba is Bengaluru's beloved community arts and culture festival featuring live performances, art pop-ups, independent music acts, creative workshops, street food, and local maker markets across the city.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: BLR Hubba is Bengaluru's indie arts and culture festival — genZScore 7-8. The crowd is creative, artsy, culturally aware, and trend-forward. Bengaluru context = experimental and fashion-forward. This is a multi-day outdoor daytime event with performances and art spaces — comfort meets cool. The aesthetic is indie-cool, artsy maximalism, and eclectic layering. Think vintage-inspired prints, statement co-ords, artsy accessories, bucket hats, tote bags, and interesting textures. Not hypebeast streetwear, not ethnic — this is the creative GenZ who references Coachella but makes it desi-indie. Colors are eclectic — earthy tones mixed with pops of color. Fusion and western both strong. Jewellery is quirky and statement — chunky resin, geometric, indie-craft.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: BLR Hubba crowd is the artsy indie GenZ — think Bengaluru creative scene meets global festival energy. Lead with statement printed co-ords, linen shirts in earthy tones, oversized tees with interesting graphics, and wide-leg trousers. Women in flowy midi dresses, abstract printed sets, or a vintage-inspired top with straight-fit jeans. Tote bags are a must — canvas or printed. Caps and bucket hats are strong accessories. Layering works well — an oversized shirt over a crop tee. Jewellery should be quirky and artisanal — geometric earrings, chunky resin rings, layered necklaces. Comfortable footwear implied — so ankle-length and above-knee hemlines work best for walking. Denim as a base layer is always relevant.

app.headline: BLR Hubba Season, Dress Accordingly.
lines: Artsy fits for the city's most creative weekend.
Linen, prints, and a tote — your Hubba starter pack.
Bengaluru's indie crowd doesn't dress basic. Neither should you.

social_media.headline: Art Festival Fit Check, Bengaluru.
lines: POV: you're at BLR Hubba and every fit is a walking art installation.
Earthy tones, statement prints, and zero corporate energy — that's the brief.

events.title: Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy Live
events.description: Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy bring their iconic musical legacy live — a night of Bollywood chart-toppers, soulful originals, and high-energy anthems spanning decades of India's most beloved film and indie music.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: SEL is a legendary Bollywood and indie music trio — their live concerts attract a wide age range but the core GenZ and millennial crowd skews at genZScore 5-7. It's a premium indoor or semi-outdoor concert night in Bengaluru — trend-forward, experimental city. The vibe is elevated casual to smart dressy — not club-level glam but definitely not basic. The Bollywood connection means people put in effort. Think stylish co-ords, satin tops, smart casuals for men, statement earrings, and a generally put-together night-out aesthetic. Nostalgia is a strong undercurrent — some Y2K and retro-Bollywood-inspired styling is totally on-brand here. Colors lean into evening palette — deep jewel tones, black, navy, burgundy with metallic or colorful accents.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: SEL live is a premium Bollywood concert night — the crowd dresses up but keeps it cool, not over-the-top. Women in satin midi dresses, stylish co-ords, or a statement top with wide-leg trousers. Men in well-fitted shirts — solid or subtle print — with smart trousers or dark jeans. Jewellery should be statement but not bridal — chunky earrings, layered necklaces, a bold ring. The nostalgia factor is real — Y2K-inspired cuts, retro-Bollywood shimmer references work beautifully here. Deep jewel tones are the evening palette. Small structured bags or clutches for women. Watches are a strong men's accessory call. Bengaluru crowd will push toward experimental cuts and interesting silhouettes over safe choices.

app.headline: SEL Night Needs A Moment.
lines: Dil Chahta Hai energy but make it fashion.
Concert fits that hit as hard as the setlist.
Your SEL era deserves the right outfit.

social_media.headline: SEL Live. Dress Like It.
lines: POV: SEL drops Breathless and your satin co-ord is doing all the heavy lifting.
Bollywood concert fits — jewel tones, statement earrings, zero basic energy.

events.title: Candlelight Tribute to KK
events.genres: Music, Live Concert, Tribute, Bollywood, Romantic
events.description: A candlelight evening tribute to the legendary singer KK, featuring live renditions of his most beloved songs in an intimate, emotion-filled setting — celebrating a voice that defined a generation.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: A candlelight tribute to KK is deeply emotional and intimate — genZScore 5-6. The audience is GenZ and millennials who grew up on KK's music — Pal, Tadap Tadap, Yaaron. The vibe is soft, romantic, and nostalgic. This is an indoor evening event with warm candlelight aesthetics — soft-girl and soft-boy energy is the dominant fashion mood. Think flowy silhouettes, soft fabric drapes, muted romantic colors — dusty rose, lavender, cream, blush, soft browns. No loud prints or clubbing energy — this is the outfit you wear when you want to feel something. Jewellery is delicate and dainty — minimal, gold-tone, subtle. Men in soft neutral tones — beige, cream, dusty blue shirts. The overall palette mirrors candlelight — warm, soft, golden.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Soft-girl and soft-boy dressing for a candlelight KK tribute — this is the outfit that feels like a hug. Women in flowy floral midi dresses, satin slip dresses in dusty rose or lavender, or a soft co-ord in cream and gold. Men in well-fitted pastel or neutral shirts — beige, dusty blue, soft white — tucked into slim trousers. Delicate jewellery is everything here — thin gold chains, small stud earrings, a simple ring stack, dainty anklets. No heavy embellishments, no loud patterns. The candlelight palette rules — warm creams, soft blush, dusty lavender, golden beige. Small structured bags or soft fabric clutches. The overall energy is quiet luxury meets emotional softness — dress like you're about to cry beautifully to Pal.

app.headline: Dress Soft. Feel Everything.
lines: KK's songs deserve your most emotional outfit.
Flowy, dreamy, candlelit — we found your look.
Soft fits for a night full of feelings.

social_media.headline: Crying Beautifully In This Outfit.
lines: POV: Pal starts playing and your dusty rose dress is doing the most.
Candlelight tribute fit check — soft colors, dainty jewels, full emotional damage.

events.title: Beat the Bull Run + Suggi Festival
events.description: A high-energy community event combining a morning bull run fitness challenge with the vibrant Suggi harvest festival — celebrating Bengaluru's unique blend of athletic spirit and Karnataka's rich cultural traditions.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: This is a dual-vibe event — genZScore 6-7. Morning fitness run meets afternoon cultural harvest festival (Suggi is a Karnataka harvest celebration). The crowd is active, community-oriented GenZ Bengaluru. Two distinct fashion moments: (1) the run — athletic, performance-wear, functional, bright and energetic; (2) the Suggi festival — Karnataka ethnic, colorful, festive but grounded. Fusion is the sweet spot for those doing both. Athleisure that transitions into casual festive works well. Colors are energetic for the run — neons, brights — then shifting to earthy harvest tones for the festival. Jewellery is minimal for the run, light ethnic pieces for the festival. The Bengaluru crowd will attempt to style both in one look.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Two-act dressing — plan for the run AND the festival. For the Bull Run: performance tees, fitted shorts or joggers in bright neons and color-blocks, running caps, and sport socks. Lightweight jackets for the Bengaluru morning chill. For Suggi festival: Karnataka's harvest aesthetic — earthy oranges, turmeric yellows, greens and reds in ethnic cotton sets or fusion kurta-with-jeans. Women can transition with a colorful dupatta or ethnic printed co-ord. Men in a casual kurta over joggers hits the fusion sweet spot. Minimal jewellery throughout — small stud earrings, a simple bracelet, or a light anklet for the festival segment. Caps and tote bags work across both halves. Kids ethnic sets and sporty tees are both relevant here.

app.headline: Run It, Then Celebrate It.
lines: Athletic fits for the run, ethnic fits for the fest.
Bengaluru doing two things at once — your wardrobe should too.
From track to Suggi in one look — we got you.

social_media.headline: Sprinted Into A Harvest Festival.
lines: POV: wore your ethnic co-ord to a bull run and somehow it worked.
Morning: neon running fit. Afternoon: turmeric ethnic set. That's the brief.

events.title: Linkin Park Live
events.genres: Music, Live Concert, Rock, Alternative, Metal
events.description: Linkin Park brings their legendary hybrid rock sound to life — a high-octane night of iconic anthems, raw energy, and an era-defining setlist that hits every emotion at full volume.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Linkin Park live is a massive deal — genZScore 8-9. The crowd spans nostalgic millennials who grew up on Hybrid Theory AND new-era GenZ fans brought in by the 2024 comeback with Emily Armstrong. The fashion energy is alt-rock, nu-metal revival, Y2K grunge, and dark streetwear. Think all-black everything, band tees, cargo pants, chunky boots implied, graphic prints, chains, and dark accessories. The Bengaluru crowd will go extra — expect ripped denim, fishnet layers, oversized band merch, dark liner energy. Colors are almost exclusively dark — black, charcoal, dark grey, burgundy, with occasional red or white graphic accents. Jewellery is edgy — chains, spikes, chunky rings, ear cuffs. This is one of the highest GenZ fashion-intensity concerts possible.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: This is the darkest, most alt-GenZ fashion moment in the calendar — dress accordingly. The uniform is black on black on black. Oversized band tees — LP or any rock band graphic — tucked into cargo pants or ripped skinny jeans. Distressed denim is a must. Women in fishnet layers under a band tee, leather-look jackets, chunky boots implied, dark co-ords or a black mini with combat-adjacent accessories. Men in oversized hoodies, cargo trousers, dark denim. Jewellery is bold and edgy — chain necklaces, chunky silver rings, ear cuffs, spike bracelets. Caps backward. Crossbody bags or sling bags only — hands need to be free for the mosh pit. Ripped and distressed denim is the single most relevant catalog category for this event. The Bengaluru crowd will go maximally alt — reward that energy.

app.headline: All Black. Full Volume. LP Night.
lines: In the end, it always comes down to the fit.
Band tee, cargo pants, chain — your LP starter pack.
Dress like the mosh pit is calling your name.

social_media.headline: Numb To Everything Except This Fit.
lines: POV: Linkin Park drops Crawling and your all-black fit ascends to another dimension.
LP concert dress code — black, distressed, chains, zero compromise.

events.title: Chrizellenz (Christ University) Begins
events.genres: College Fest, Cultural, Music, Fashion, Campus
events.description: Chrizellenz is Christ University Bengaluru's flagship annual college festival — a multi-day celebration of music, dance, fashion, art, and campus culture bringing together students from across the city for competitions, performances, and non-stop college energy.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Christ University college fest — genZScore 8-9. This is peak campus GenZ fashion energy in Bengaluru. The crowd is 18-22, trend-aware, social-media-native, and dressing to be seen. College fests in Bengaluru are serious fashion moments — Instagram fits, OOTD energy, and competitive dressing. The vibe spans multiple moods across days — day 1 casual-cool, later days escalate to party and performance energy. Think Y2K, streetwear, co-ords, bold prints, crop tops, low-rise, baggy jeans, statement accessories. Colors are maximally vibrant and eclectic. Fashion show events within the fest push even more experimental looks. Both western and fusion work — desi streetwear fusion is very on-trend for this crowd. Jewellery is statement and layered.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Christ University fest is Bengaluru's campus fashion olympics — everyone is dressed to be photographed. Day 1 energy: casual-cool streetwear, oversized graphic tees, baggy jeans, fresh sneakers implied, caps. Later days escalate — co-ords, crop tops, Y2K low-rise skirts, wide-leg trousers, statement sets. Women in bold printed co-ords, colorful mini dresses, or a crop top with high-waist jeans. Men in oversized fits, colour-blocked shirts, cargo trousers. Desi fusion is strong here — ethnic printed crop tops with jeans, or a bandhej co-ord styled streetwear. Jewellery is stacked and maximalist — evil eye chains, alphabet pendants, zodiac rings, butterfly clips. Bags are mini and crossbody. Caps, bucket hats, and bold sunglasses are key accessories. Distressed denim is always relevant. The fashion show events within the fest will push more experimental editorial looks.

app.headline: Christ Fest. No Basic Fits Allowed.
lines: Chrizellenz season means your OOTD game better be serious.
Co-ords, crop tops, or a whole moment — pick your weapon.
Campus fits that survive the Instagram audit.

social_media.headline: Chrizellenz Fit Check, Go.
lines: POV: walked into Christ fest and every outfit was a whole editorial.
College fest dress code — loud prints, stacked jewellery, zero repeats.

events.title: Basant Panchami
events.description: Basant Panchami marks the arrival of spring and is dedicated to Goddess Saraswati — celebrated with prayers, yellow-clad festivities, kite flying, and the joyful welcoming of the new season across North and East India.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Basant Panchami is a deeply color-coded festival — genZScore 2-4. Yellow is non-negotiable, it IS the festival. Dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, it's a family and community celebration — primarily women and students dressing in yellow ethnic wear. The mood is joyful, spring-fresh, and devotional. Sarees, salwar suits, and ethnic sets in yellow, saffron, and mustard are the dominant look. Men in yellow kurtas or at minimum a yellow accent. The spring arrival means lighter fabrics — cotton, linen, chiffon-feel. Jewellery is traditional but not too heavy — gold-tone pieces that complement yellow beautifully. Some GenZ fusion is possible — yellow ethnic co-ords or a yellow printed top with ethnic accessories. No western, no dark colors.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Yellow is the entire brief — no exceptions. Lead with yellow sarees in cotton or satin-feel with gold zari borders for women. Salwar suits and ethnic sets in mustard, saffron, and turmeric tones are equally strong. Men in yellow or mustard kurta-pyjama — even a yellow kurta with white pyjama is iconic for this day. Lighter spring fabrics are key — cotton, linen, rayon over heavy silks. Gold-tone jewellery complements yellow perfectly — jhumkas, bangles, simple necklaces. The spring energy calls for floral embroidery and botanical prints as secondary pattern accents. GenZ fusion angle — a yellow bandhej or printed co-ord with ethnic accessories hits beautifully. Kids ethnic sets in yellow are highly relevant. Avoid any dark or western pieces — this festival has a strict color code that makes it uniquely shoppable.

app.headline: Yellow Is The Only Vibe Today.
lines: Basant Panchami has one rule — wear yellow, look divine.
Mustard saree or saffron suit — both are correct answers.
Spring is here and your ethnic wardrobe knows it.

social_media.headline: Yellow Fit Or Wrong Fit.
lines: POV: showed up to Saraswati Puja in a mustard silk saree and the goddess approved.
Basant Panchami dress code — yellow, gold, and absolutely nothing else.

events.title: Republic Day
events.description: Republic Day marks the adoption of India's Constitution on 26th January 1950 — celebrated with grand parades, flag hoisting ceremonies, cultural performances, and a deep sense of national pride across the country.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Republic Day is a national holiday — genZScore 3-5. The crowd spans all ages — school children in uniforms, families at parade grounds, young adults watching from home or attending local events. The dominant fashion signal is patriotic — tricolor palette of saffron, white, and green/navy. Two distinct styling moods: (1) formal-patriotic for parade and flag hoisting — crisp whites, ethnic formal, salwar suits, kurtas; (2) casual patriotic for the day off — relaxed Indian flag color outfits, ethnic casual, fusion. Ethnic and fusion both work strongly. Western with patriotic color-blocking is very GenZ. Jewellery is moderate — tricolor accessories, simple gold pieces. The Bengaluru GenZ crowd will find creative ways to do patriotic fashion without being costume-y.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Patriotic dressing done right — the tricolor is the palette but it doesn't have to be a costume. Women in crisp white kurtas with saffron or green dupattas is the classic power move. Sarees in white with zari or embroidered borders in saffron and green are stunning. Men in white or off-white kurta-pyjama — clean, proud, effortless. GenZ fusion angle — a white oversized shirt with orange and green color-blocked co-ord, or a tricolor printed tee with white trousers. Chikankari white kurtas are incredibly relevant for this day — delicate, elegant, and Indian. Kids ethnic sets in white and orange are highly shoppable. Jewellery should be simple and gold-toned or tricolor-inspired. Caps with Indian motifs are a strong accessories call. The overall energy is proud, clean, and put-together — not overly festive, not casual.

app.headline: Dress Proud This Republic Day.
lines: Saffron, white, green — your most patriotic palette awaits.
White kurta energy hits different on January 26th.
Tricolor fits that actually look good, not costume-y.

social_media.headline: Republic Day Fit, No Cap.
lines: POV: showed up in a white chikankari kurta and the whole parade noticed.
Tricolor dressing — saffron top, white pants, green dupatta. Constitution approved.

events.title: BIFFes Begins
events.genres: Film Festival, Arts & Culture, Cinema, Independent
events.description: BIFFes — the Bengaluru International Film Festival — returns with a curated selection of world cinema, indie films, documentaries, and masterclasses, drawing cinephiles, filmmakers, and cultural enthusiasts from across the country.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: BIFFes is Bengaluru's premier film festival — genZScore 6-7. The crowd is intellectual, artistic, culturally curious GenZ and millennials — film students, indie directors, cinephiles, writers. This is not a glam red-carpet event — it's a cerebral, cool, understated fashion moment. Think quiet luxury meets bookish artsy. The aesthetic is intentional and considered — interesting textures, muted earth tones, vintage-inspired silhouettes, linen and cotton layers, statement glasses, and tote bags carrying film festival schedules. No loud prints or clubbing energy. Colors are muted, sophisticated — olive, beige, grey, tan, rust, cream, deep navy. Jewellery is minimal and artisanal. The Bengaluru cinephile crowd dresses like they've seen every Wes Anderson film and it shows.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: BIFFes crowd dresses like the films they watch — intentional, layered, and quietly interesting. Lead with linen shirts in olive, beige, or cream for men — tucked or half-tucked into straight trousers or chinos. Women in wide-leg linen trousers with a solid turtleneck, a relaxed midi dress in earthy tones, or an oversized blazer over a simple tee. Layering is key — the Bengaluru January cool makes it perfect for interesting textile combinations. Statement glasses are the single most important accessory for this crowd — bold frames, round tortoiseshell, or vintage-inspired. Tote bags are non-negotiable — canvas or printed. Jewellery is minimal and artisanal — a simple chain, small stud earrings, one interesting ring. Vintage-inspired checks or subtle stripe shirts work beautifully. The overall energy is effortless cool — looks like they didn't try but absolutely did.

app.headline: Film Festival Fits Hit Different.
lines: Cinephile dressing — linen, earth tones, statement glasses.
Your BIFFes OOTD should feel like an indie film still.
Dress like you have opinions about the French New Wave.

social_media.headline: Dressed Like A Film Festival Person.
lines: POV: walked into BIFFes in an olive linen shirt and everyone assumed you made a documentary.
BIFFes fit check — earth tones, tote bag, glasses that say I have taste.

events.title: New Year's Day
events.description: Ring in the New Year with parties, fireworks, music, and celebrations as the world toasts to new beginnings.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: New Year's Day spans two moods — the wild NYE night bleeding into Jan 1st brunches and hangout vibes. Primary crowd is GenZ and millennials going from party mode to chill recovery fits. Night energy = sequins, shimmer, metallics. Day-after = elevated casual, co-ords, trendy loungewear. Jewellery is maximalist. Colors lean into midnight black, gold, silver, champagne, and bold reds. Fusion and western both work — no strong ethnic pull unless it's a family lunch context.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: NYE-to-New-Year energy — lead with sequin minis, satin slip dresses, and metallic co-ords for women. Men in tailored shirts, smart trousers, or a sleek black set. Maximalist jewellery is a must — stack rings, layer necklaces, chunky earrings. Bags should be small and embellished — clutches and mini bags. Gold and silver are the accent colors of the night. Day-after fits shift to elevated casual — oversized blazers, wide-leg trousers, trendy tops.

app.headline: New Year, Same You But Hotter
lines: Your countdown outfit isn't going to pick itself.
Sequins, satin, or a sleek co-ord — we've got all three.
Dress like the year is already yours.

social_media.headline: New Year Who? Dressed Era.
lines: POV: you actually planned your NYE fit this time.
Gold sequins at midnight > resolutions you won't keep.

events.title: Sakat Chauth
events.description: Sakat Chauth is a Hindu festival where mothers observe a day-long fast for the well-being and long life of their children, breaking it after sighting the moon in the evening.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Sakat Chauth is a traditional Hindu religious observance — primarily women, specifically mothers. genZScore is 1-3. The vibe is devotional, traditional, family-first. Women dress in ethnic wear — sarees, salwar suits, ethnic sets — in auspicious colors like red, yellow, orange, and green. Evening moon-sighting adds a slightly elevated occasion layer but stays fully ethnic. Jewellery is traditional and meaningful — gold tones, temple-style, no western influence. Fusion is minimal, western is irrelevant here.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Full ethnic, full devotion. Lead with sarees in silk-feel or cotton-blend with zari borders — red and yellow are the most auspicious. Salwar suits and ethnic sets in embroidered or self-design fabrics work beautifully. Jewellery is the star — gold-toned necklaces, jhumkas, bangles, and maang tikka if possible. Dupatta is essential as a styling layer. Avoid anything western or fusion-heavy. Evening moon-sighting calls for a slightly more elevated look — think silk sarees or embellished ethnic sets.

app.headline: Dressed For The Moon Tonight
lines: Fast all day, look stunning at moonrise.
Your most auspicious outfit is one tap away.
Sarees, suits, and all the gold you deserve.

social_media.headline: Vrat Vibes, Full Glam.
lines: She fasted all day AND showed up in a silk saree. Respect.
Moon didn't rise yet but your outfit already did.
